164303.explanation: The municipality encourages local initiatives. It is important that the plan is feasible and contributes to a better and livable neighborhood.

164304.explanation: No party supports taxation purely for the sake of taxation. Lowering taxes puts pressure on amenities in our city, such as the library, the theater, community squares, and the swimming pool.

164305.explanation: Our city does well if the region does well. That is why we collaborate with other municipalities. We do this in areas such as housing, social security, health, safety, and energy. This way, we combine strengths, which benefits our residents.

164306.explanation: We place great value on the input and involvement of our residents in local issues. We are exploring new forms of participation, such as a permanent citizen council.

164307.explanation: It is important that municipal services are accessible and easy to reach for everyone. This can be done by adjusting opening hours in the evenings and weekends. First, the exact needs must be determined.

164309.explanation: A dog tax creates extra costs for residents with dogs without contributing much to animal welfare, safety, or cleanliness. Meanwhile, dogs contribute to exercise, reduced loneliness, and neighborhood contacts for many people.

164310.explanation: Nieuwegein is an attractive city for businesses. We are proud of that. We already do a lot to attract businesses, better utilize existing office buildings and business parks, and ensure employment. This doesn't need to cost much money.

164311.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA is not in favor of further expanding business parks. That is also unnecessary. Space is scarce in our municipality and needed for housing, green areas, and living space.

164312.explanation: We support residents who jointly generate their own green and affordable electricity with wind turbines or solar panels. They use the power in the same place, so the grid is not burdened. Energy cooperatives are an asset to our municipality and should be encouraged.

164314.explanation: Nieuwegein must be and remain an attractive place, also for our youth. Strengthening the nightlife can certainly contribute to that. It is important, however, to limit nuisance for neighbors and maintain public order.

164316.explanation: This is not the municipality’s responsibility. These are agreements between landlord and tenant. The municipality can encourage landlords to charge affordable rent. There must be enough opportunities for small businesses. This helps prevent vacancies and contributes to a varied offering.

164317.explanation: We explicitly focus on strengthening and expanding greenery in our city. We do this by planting at least one tree for every tree that is removed.

164318.explanation: We want a healthy city with clean air. Burning wood is harmful to health and causes air pollution.

164319.explanation: We stimulate businesses that focus on the future. Priority is given to companies that rapidly move away from fossil energy.

164320.explanation: We consider a clean and healthy city important. Free bulky waste collection can contribute to this. It is even better if people bring their waste themselves, as the municipality can then separate it more effectively.

164321.explanation: This way residents can more easily bring their waste and items, even if they work during the week. We believe that waste separation and recycling should be as accessible as possible to encourage a sustainable municipality.

164322.explanation: This decision was made by the municipal council in 2024. From the perspective of reliable governance, this plan will be executed and existing legal procedures will be followed.

164323.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes that residents should receive support from the municipality if their plan contributes to a green and livable neighborhood. More greenery looks attractive, provides cooling when it is warm, and is good for biodiversity.

164325.explanation: Reducing greenhouse gases is important to combat climate change and guarantee a healthy future for everyone. The municipality must therefore invest in sustainable energy, green buildings, and clean mobility.

164327.explanation: Status holders need a safe and stable place to live quickly in order to integrate. GroenLinks-PvdA believes that prioritization helps them participate well in society and offers equal opportunities.

164328.explanation: Integration is a shared responsibility. Good integration enables newcomers to participate in society.

164329.explanation: Nieuwegein must take responsibility in sheltering vulnerable people who need protection. When the situation demands it, it is possible to accommodate extra asylum seekers, provided it fits within the municipality’s capacity and facilities can be arranged responsibly.

164331.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA considers it important that every child has the chance to take music lessons. Cultural development in children is important. We want music lessons to be accessible to children from low-income families as well. This way, we offer every child equal opportunities.

164332.explanation: We find it important that all children can play sports and/or take music lessons. Therefore, we invest more in our youth. The municipality pays extra attention to children from lower-income families so that they especially have equal opportunities to participate in sports and music lessons.

164336.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is important, but we do not believe cameras alone are the right solution. Your privacy is a great good that should not be compromised lightly. Therefore, we prefer more enforcement officers on the street who can intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

164337.explanation: Violence against our enforcement officers (BOAs) is unacceptable. In Nieuwegein, BOAs already wear bodycams to make their work safer and increase the feeling of safety. The BOAs view this positively. We do not believe safety is increased by providing more weapons. The monopoly on violence remains with the police.

164341.explanation: There is a great shortage of affordable housing. Residents have to wait far too long for a home. GroenLinks-PvdA wants to build more affordable homes. In new construction, 40% should be social rental housing. This way, we ensure that at least 30% of all homes in the city are social rentals.

164343.explanation: Converting vacant offices into homes is a solution to the housing shortage, as it counters vacancy and provides more affordable housing. We have already converted many offices.

164344.explanation: A coffeeshop allows controlled use of soft drugs and can regulate nuisance, reduce crime, and improve supervision. At the same time, it raises concerns about livability and health. Strong focus on safety, health, and living environment is necessary.

164345.explanation: We encourage sharing and splitting homes. We do this through projects like 'Rooms with Opportunities.' This offers youths aged 16 to 26 the chance to learn to live independently through coaching in all areas of life.

164348.explanation: All homes in the Netherlands must be disconnected from natural gas. We are switching to clean energy. This way, Nieuwegein remains a pleasant place to live. It is important that this energy is affordable for everyone. The national government should allocate more funds to ensure housing cost neutrality.

164349.explanation: We want to prevent residents from falling into poverty. By raising the income threshold, more residents receive timely assistance and we prevent greater financial problems.

164350.explanation: We want to put an end to the culture of mistrust in social assistance. People receiving social benefits have the right to support without any obligations attached. The municipality must provide guidance and opportunities so that everyone can participate in society at their own pace.

164351.explanation: We have gained some small-scale experience with basic jobs in Nieuwegein. A basic job is a paid position for people who find it difficult or impossible to find work.

164355.explanation: We consider a livable, healthy, and sustainable environment important. Clean transportation contributes to this. The municipality’s fleet, buses, and trucks should be as clean as possible. This also reduces noise pollution. A low-emission zone could be an option in the future.

164356.explanation: Parking is never free. Someone has to pay for parking garages. We believe that visitors to the garage should contribute to the costs. Additionally, the municipality should promote walking, cycling, and public transport.

164357.explanation: The data clearly show that lower speeds on neighborhood roads make traffic much safer. For vulnerable road users like cyclists, this makes a world of difference. Therefore, the maximum speed on neighborhood roads is 30 km/h. Different speeds apply on main roads.

164362.explanation: Everyone should be able to use public transport. Free public transport based solely on age is not targeted. It is fairer to focus support on low-income groups so that help reaches those who really need it.

164363.explanation: Bicycle mobility plays an important role in Nieuwegein. We shape this, among other ways, by converting more streets into bicycle streets, where cars are guests. This keeps cycling safe on neighborhood roads while cars use the main roads.

164364.explanation: Het Klooster is conveniently located along the highway and is therefore already easily accessible by car. We also want the area to be well accessible by bike and public transport.

164367.explanation: We encourage supermarkets and hospitality businesses to offer healthy products. We oppose advertising for unhealthy products. This makes healthy choices easier and discourages unhealthy ones.

164303.explanation: We believe there should be room for neighborhood culture and social encounters. Neighborhood culture budgets should be available for multicultural festivals, film evenings, and language or talent programs. Subsidies should focus on small-scale, diverse, and accessible initiatives that bring residents together.

164304.explanation: If it turns out to be possible, it should certainly happen. After all, our residents are not cash machines! If there is structurally extra money, it should be returned to residents, for example by reducing housing costs (think of lowering property tax and waste and sewage charges).

164305.explanation: Collaboration is good if the municipality and residents benefit. Since there are already many partnerships, our aim is not to enter into many more. Nieuwegein is large enough to carry out certain tasks independently. Collaboration can sometimes also cause delays.

164306.explanation: We support citizen participation: the more the better. In our view, this can happen through multiple channels but certainly also through the citizens' assembly.

164307.explanation: The counter is there to serve our residents. Therefore, opening hours should be aligned with the needs of our residents.

164309.explanation: The dog tax is an old and unfair tax.

164310.explanation: Absolutely! Nieuwegein is also a nice city to work in and that should be promoted!

164311.explanation: Entrepreneurs should be able to start or expand more quickly. Therefore, we advocate for more flexible zoning plans and less bureaucracy with permits so that good initiatives are not unnecessarily blocked.

164312.explanation: There are many schemes for sustainability, but not everyone knows about them. The municipality must actively communicate about these opportunities so more residents can insulate their homes and save energy. The municipality should stimulate such initiatives, including financially.

164314.explanation: We believe there should be something to do for everyone in Nieuwegein: for young and old. If young people want a certain type of hospitality venue, the municipality should cooperate and facilitate an appropriate location.

164316.explanation: We want to counter vacancies and rent out municipal properties as optimally as possible, also in the context of employment and entrepreneurship.

164317.explanation: Green should remain green. Nieuwegein is a green city, and we want to keep it that way. Green contributes to a better environment and to residents' well-being.

164318.explanation: Consulting the heating guide is sufficient to handle this responsibly.

164319.explanation: We support stimulating sustainable businesses but oppose excluding companies outright.

164320.explanation: A clean city feels more pleasant and safe. We tackle illegal dumping of waste and want to abolish the fee for bulky waste collection to prevent illegal dumping. There will also be periodic free bulky waste collections.

164321.explanation: This fits with better service to residents. A clean city feels more pleasant and safe. We tackle illegal waste dumping and want to abolish the bulky waste collection fee to prevent illegal dumping. Also, there will be periodic free bulky waste collections.

164322.explanation: We support activities for everyone. There are many great ideas but often no suitable place can be found. We wish to use the event terrain for these diverse activities.

164323.explanation: Existing subsidies for this should be maintained.

164325.explanation: This can certainly be the goal, but we must be honest (everyone considers 2040 unachievable) and realize that investment is needed in certain areas (housing construction), even if this does not contribute directly to this ambition.

164327.explanation: As a municipality, we must do what is required and take responsibility for housing status holders. But giving them priority for rental homes over others who have been on the waiting list for a long time is not necessary.

164328.explanation: The municipality must put more effort and time into the integration process and better monitor whether people do what they should. It can be better and must be done together, with each other, with the city/neighborhood/street. The funds needed for this must come from the national government.

164329.explanation: We support a fair distribution of asylum seeker shelter. So Nieuwegein houses asylum seekers, but so do the surrounding municipalities. The COA should first approach municipalities that do little or nothing.

164331.explanation: Children must be able to develop and flourish, regardless of their family's financial situation. Making music lessons possible for every child fits our vision.

164332.explanation: We support equal opportunities for all children. All children up to 14 years old must be able to do sports or play music regardless of family income. Therefore, we propose providing an annual voucher of €100 per child. A healthy youth is the foundation of a healthy city!

164336.explanation: We think more camera surveillance is needed! Cameras are an important tool to combat crime and nuisance. We want camera surveillance to be used more often and more targeted at serious safety issues such as drug trafficking, money laundering in industrial areas, and waste dumping.

164337.explanation: This can be an important issue for safety. Evaluate the powers of the municipal enforcement officers (boa’s). In 2025, boa’s received more powers. This expansion must be evaluated and, if necessary, adjusted so boa’s can do their work effectively and carefully.

164341.explanation: We support a percentage of 30%, not 40%. There must be room for everyone in Nieuwegein, including middle- and high-income groups.

164343.explanation: The municipality must cooperate to realize this, facilitate it, and contribute financially where necessary to achieve the desired number of affordable homes.

164344.explanation: Not mandatory, but allowed if the requested location is suitable.

164345.explanation: We see renting out spaces as a (temporary) solution to the housing shortage. It can also be a welcome solution for people who feel lonely or who might need some form of support.

164348.explanation: In any case, this must not cost residents any extra money!

164349.explanation: We believe you should not wait until people are in trouble, but try to prevent problems. Raising a threshold so that residents receive help sooner works in our opinion.

164350.explanation: In our view, we must keep the human scale in mind: small help from neighbors or family, such as a bag of groceries, is not fraud. On the other hand, recipients of social assistance are expected to provide a reasonable counter-performance in exchange for support.

164351.explanation: Sheltered workplaces (formerly social employment) should be offered as much as possible within Nieuwegein itself, so residents can continue working in their familiar environment.

164355.explanation: Everyone must be able to reach the center of Nieuwegein.

164356.explanation: As far as we're concerned, the first 2 hours should be free. This works wonderfully in places like IJsselstein and Houten!

164357.explanation: The statement is too simplistic and does not cover the issue. We support a combined application: on many roads the maximum speed is 30 km/h, but on some 50 km/h is also allowed.

164362.explanation: We think it's important that everyone can remain mobile. The measure to make public transport free for those aged 67+ contributes to this.

164363.explanation: Roads like Jutphasestraatweg and Vreeswijksestraatweg need to be made safer. These routes are busy but run along residential areas. We want traffic here to be calmer and more transparent, with better protection for cyclists and pedestrians.

164364.explanation: The Klooster should be well accessible by car and public transport.

164367.explanation: We support a healthy lifestyle but oppose banning such advertisements.

164303.explanation: Residents know their neighborhood best and can play an important role in the maintenance and greening of their living environment. That is why the VVD believes these initiatives should be encouraged. This strengthens the bond between neighbors and makes our neighborhoods more livable.

164304.explanation: The VVD supports low local taxes. This keeps living and doing business affordable. The municipality must budget carefully and work efficiently instead of raising taxes. At the same time, the VVD believes that important facilities (theater, library, swimming pool) must be maintained.

164305.explanation: The municipality must weigh whether it is worthwhile to do things themselves or together with others. What comes first is what is best for the residents of Nieuwegein. In many cases, doing things together is cheaper and more efficient.

164306.explanation: Setting up a citizens' council requires a lot of money and effort from the municipality. Therefore, the VVD believes the municipality should be cautious about this.

164307.explanation: To further improve municipal services, the VVD believes the City Hall should be open more often outside office hours for matters that cannot be arranged digitally or by phone, such as picking up a passport or driver’s license.

164309.explanation: The VVD believes local taxes should be kept as low as possible. We consider dog tax an unfair extra burden for dog owners.

164310.explanation: The VVD believes we should continue promoting Nieuwegein, also for entrepreneurs. It strengthens the city's image, supports existing entrepreneurs, and attracts new businesses. It increases the city's liveliness and economic growth.

164311.explanation: The VVD aims for a good climate for entrepreneurs. Where business expansion is desired, the municipality should cooperate. We know very well that we have very little space for this expansion.

164312.explanation: As long as it is voluntary, affordable, and effective. This way, we encourage local initiative and innovation without coercion.

164314.explanation: Nieuwegein could use more nightlife options. That also includes night catering in a suitable location.

164316.explanation: The owner of City Plaza is a publicly listed investor; financial support from the government (and thus from our residents' tax money) is prohibited.

164317.explanation: The VVD values a green living environment. This can look different in every part of the city. The VVD considers the mandatory one-to-one replacement of every felled tree to be symbolic politics.

164318.explanation: Nothing is cozier than a crackling wood fire. That should remain. The municipality can actively inform residents about the existence of the Fire Guide.

164319.explanation: As far as the VVD is concerned, every entrepreneur in Nieuwegein who adds economic and social value to our city is welcome. The government should be cautious with additional rules that obstruct.

164320.explanation: According to the VVD, this sounds attractive but increases costs and taxes. The collection fee for residents should only cover costs. According to the VVD, free collection does not lead to less illegal dumping.

164321.explanation: Extending the opening hours of the recycling station helps keep the city clean. Many people work during the week and for example, have sports appointments on Saturdays.

164322.explanation: Nieuwegein could use more vibrancy. An event site contributes to this. Over the past years, the VVD has worked hard to realize this.

164323.explanation: Residents know their neighborhood best and can play an important role in the maintenance and greening of their living environment. That is why the VVD believes these initiatives should be encouraged. This strengthens the bond between neighbors and makes our neighborhoods more livable.

164325.explanation: The VVD believes climate goals must be realistic. We support sustainability and reducing greenhouse gases, but only if it is feasible and affordable.

164327.explanation: Everyone has the right to suitable housing. We prefer a priority rule for housing-seeking residents of Nieuwegein and employees in certain sectors such as care, safety, and education.

164328.explanation: The VVD believes newcomers must integrate quickly and learn Dutch promptly. This allows newcomers to work and participate in our society as soon as possible. No funds should go to initiatives that undermine integration or our values.

164329.explanation: The VVD believes Nieuwegein must comply with national agreements. If all municipalities do this, there is no need for us to accommodate more people than agreed.

164331.explanation: The VVD considers cultural education important for children, but it is more than just music lessons. With the subsidies the municipality provides to cultural institutions, providers can keep prices moderate, and the city pass also offers opportunities.

164332.explanation: We have multiple options in Nieuwegein (such as the city pass) to participate affordably in sports and cultural activities.

164336.explanation: Nieuwegein seldom uses camera surveillance. The VVD believes camera surveillance is an important tool for safety and enforcement. Cameras may be used targeted, for example, in socially unsafe places and locations where waste is dumped.

164337.explanation: We see society is becoming harsher. Enforcement officers face conflicts on the front line and must be well equipped for these situations, also for their own safety.

164341.explanation: The VVD considers 40% too high. This hinders housing flow. We choose mixed housing with room for mid-range rent and homeownership so people can move up and neighborhoods remain livable.

164343.explanation: Nieuwegein has been a frontrunner for years in transforming office buildings. The VVD believes these small studios are often of poor quality. Also, owners of office buildings earn a lot from these projects, so subsidies are not necessary.

164344.explanation: In the past, a coffeeshop in Nieuwegein caused major nuisance for neighbors. We do not want that again. Furthermore, a coffeeshop has opened in Westraven that is easily accessible for Nieuwegein residents.

164345.explanation: Given the housing shortage, it should be possible to rent out, for example, a room in your house. The VVD believes this must be well monitored to prevent nuisance in the neighborhood if multiple homes are split.

164348.explanation: Together, we have the responsibility to stop using natural gas. This does not necessarily have to lead to a financial benefit. The VVD does find it important that the transition is affordable for everyone.

164349.explanation: Fortunately, Nieuwegein has very generous poverty schemes. In addition, the municipality is facing tough financial times. What the VVD considers important is that people who truly need this help are always assisted.

164350.explanation: The VVD believes it is important that everyone contributes to our society. Everyone can do that in their own way. We also ask people who receive benefits to contribute.

164351.explanation: The VVD believes it is important for the long-term unemployed to get to work. For many of these people, a subsidized job is not a sustainable solution. We want to invest in support through training, guidance, and cooperation with local entrepreneurs to get people employed.

164355.explanation: The layout of our city is not suitable for an environmental zone. This affects local entrepreneurs while the environmental effect is limited. You achieve sustainability through innovation and freedom of choice, not with bans.

164356.explanation: This lowers any potential barrier to visiting City Plaza and supports local entrepreneurs. The VVD does believe that the costs should be shared with the commercial owner of the shopping center and the retailers' association.

164357.explanation: The road network in Nieuwegein is designed for a speed regime of 30 km/h in neighborhoods, 50 km/h on neighborhood roads, and 70 km/h on four-lane roads. The VVD believes the municipality should carefully consider traffic safety. Public transport should also be able to run smoothly.

164362.explanation: According to the VVD, 'free' does not exist. We choose affordable public transport for everyone and targeted support for those who really need it.

164363.explanation: The VVD stands for a municipality where all forms of transport can find their way safely and smoothly. Freedom of choice in mobility and accessibility must be leading. Bicycle streets are not an end in themselves for the VVD but can be a solution in certain locations.

164364.explanation: The Klooster is excellently accessible by car. The VVD does believe this area should be better accessible by public transport.

164367.explanation: The VVD considers this patronizing; it limits entrepreneurial freedom and has little proven effect. Healthy behavior is achieved with education and freedom of choice, not municipal bans.

164303.explanation: D66 appreciates initiatives from residents. Together we make the city. Good plans from the neighborhood should be able to receive support and resources from the municipality so that residents can truly execute their ideas.

164304.explanation: We pay for things everyone enjoys from the property tax (OZB), such as street lighting, bike paths, sidewalks, schools, the library, and greenery in the city. D66 wants the OZB to remain low and stable, but not even lower. Healthy finances and a pleasant living environment are more important.

164305.explanation: Collaborating with other municipalities is often smart and cheaper, for example for waste collection or youth assistance. D66 wants to do this where possible, but only if it is clearly arranged, saves money, and democratic oversight is well organized.

164306.explanation: D66 wants to deploy citizens' assemblies. A randomly selected group of residents receives good information, consults, and advises the municipal council on difficult topics. This way, decisions gain broader support. With citizens' assemblies, D66 reduces the gap between policy and what residents experience.

164307.explanation: D66 believes extended opening hours fit this time. It helps people who work, care for others, or cannot come during office hours. The service desk is already open until 8 pm on Wednesdays. If evening or weekend openings help residents better and are feasible, D66 supports that.

164309.explanation: For D66 this is not an issue. We see no reason to reintroduce it. However, D66 believes dog owners must clean up dog waste so our city remains clean and pleasant.

164310.explanation: Nieuwegein has much to offer and is building a lively city center. D66 wants companies in the center and other parts of the city that strengthen Nieuwegein. Therefore, we want to promote the city specifically as a sustainable, well-accessible place for entrepreneurs.

164311.explanation: D66 wants a strong local economy and therefore opts for sustainability and smart use of existing business parks. We want to improve what is already there first, rather than developing new sites.

164312.explanation: D66 gladly helps residents who want to generate sustainable energy themselves. Together, we work toward a green, sustainable city with clean energy by 2040.

164314.explanation: D66 wants Nieuwegein to be lively in the evenings and nights. Therefore, we are open to initiatives like nightlife and a nightclub so there are more activities for residents.

164316.explanation: D66 wants to strengthen City Plaza, but not by subsidizing rent reductions. We choose measures that make the center more attractive and future-proof, not support for property owners. However, D66 does want to help startups start a new business.

164317.explanation: D66 considers greenery very important. We want at least one new tree planted for every tree that disappears. Furthermore, D66 wants to preserve the greenery in Nieuwegein, better connect it, and expand where possible.

164318.explanation: D66 believes everyone should enjoy an open fireplace, barbecue, or wood stove. We do ask to be considerate of neighbors and only burn when air quality allows it. There are other ways to reduce nuisance as well. For example, see the burning guide.

164319.explanation: D66 wants Nieuwegein to be a sustainable and clean city by 2040. Companies already in the city are taking steps toward sustainability. From new companies, D66 expects them to start sustainably right away. That is also good for their business.

164320.explanation: Large waste can be brought free of charge to the municipal yard. D66 wants less litter and illegal dumping, with better facilities such as a recycling station open on Sundays as well. We encourage reuse but do not want a free home pickup service. Residents can also use a free electric cargo bike.

164321.explanation: D66 finds it logical that the recycling station can also be open on Sundays. This makes it easier for residents to dispose of and better separate their waste.

164322.explanation: D66 wants a lively city with more space for events for young and old. Research shows that Park Oudegein is the best place for an event site. D66 supports this, but the site must fit well with the other functions of the park.

164323.explanation: D66 appreciates green initiatives from residents. Together we make the city. Therefore, D66 believes that plans for neighborhood, facade, and rooftop gardens should receive financial support from the municipality where necessary.

164325.explanation: D66 wants clean air in Nieuwegein. This is important for our health and to leave the planet well for future generations. D66 wants Nieuwegein to be completely climate neutral by 2050. Therefore, we support strong measures to significantly reduce emissions well before 2040.

164327.explanation: Everyone remembers the images of people having to sleep outside on the grass in Ter Apel. D66 wants to prevent the asylum reception from getting stuck. Therefore, status holders entitled to housing must be able to move on quickly. This allows them to build a new life and participate in Nieuwegein.

164328.explanation: D66 wants newcomers to learn the language quickly and participate in Nieuwegein. Therefore, we focus on pre-integration schools and good assistance in finding suitable work. D66 actively invests in integration so newcomers can participate from day one.

164329.explanation: D66 believes Nieuwegein must be solidary with people fleeing war or persecution. We can do more than required, like other municipalities. D66 wants to distribute newcomers fairly across the city and invests in integration so they can participate from day one.

164331.explanation: D66 finds music important for children's development. It helps their brains, motor skills, and creativity. Children who want music lessons but whose parents cannot afford it should be supported by D66.

164332.explanation: D66 values music and sports highly. We want a strong offering of clubs and music lessons. All children should be able to participate. Therefore, we do not give money to everyone but help the children who otherwise could not join due to financial problems.

164336.explanation: D66 wants camera surveillance only where it is truly needed: targeted, temporary, and only in places with a lot of nuisance or problems.

164337.explanation: D66 wants enforcement officers (boa’s) to supervise effectively and visibly. They currently have a radio, handcuffs, and a bodycam. D66 does not want extra weapons such as a baton or pepper spray. According to D66, these belong to the police, who hold the monopoly on violence.

164341.explanation: D66 wants to build many more affordable homes. We make strict agreements with housing corporations and developers, with 30–40% affordable rent or purchase as the norm. At the same time, we want a mix of housing for every life phase, so Nieuwegein remains accessible to everyone.

164343.explanation: Many offices have already been converted into homes. D66 wants vacant offices to be converted into housing, provided the neighborhood can handle it. But D66 does not believe municipal subsidies should automatically be required for this.

164344.explanation: Drugs are also used in Nieuwegein. D66 is positive about a coffeeshop in Nieuwegein but only in a visible and safe location. It should be part of a realistic and well-regulated drug policy.

164345.explanation: D66 believes renting rooms to students should be possible. But too many residents in one house can cause nuisance. Therefore, D66 wants to assess per situation whether house sharing fits and is responsible.

164348.explanation: D66 wants Nieuwegein to stop using natural gas. We believe it is important that residents receive good support during this transition, so that switching remains affordable and feasible for as many people as possible.

164349.explanation: For the past one and a half years, there has been a new way to measure poverty, with different thresholds per household type. D66 wants poverty policy to remain targeted. We will only raise the income limit if it clearly helps significantly more families and produces real results.

164350.explanation: D66 believes that a work requirement for welfare benefits should not be mandatory. People need help for various reasons. However, D66 wants to look at each person individually to see how they can be guided towards (volunteer) work if that is helpful.

164351.explanation: D66 sees that work can help against loneliness and give more meaning. Therefore, we want long-term unemployed people to get extra opportunities. Residents who have been sidelined for a long time, D66 wants to help find work that suits them, so they can also participate.

164355.explanation: D66 wants cleaner air and safer traffic by making cycling, public transport, and electric driving more attractive. Because of earlier choices, such as the closure on Noordstedeweg and paid parking, traffic in the center is already limited. According to D66, a low-emission zone is therefore not necessary.

164356.explanation: D66 wants more people to cycle to City. Therefore, we will provide enough free and secure bicycle parking facilities. Car parking should remain affordable, but not free.

164357.explanation: D66 wants a maximum speed of 30 km/h in residential areas where many people cycle (built-up areas), such as Batauweg and Buizerdlaan. On larger roads, such as A.C. Verhoefweg, Wijkslooterweg, and ’s Gravenhoutseweg, speeds of 50 or 70 km/h will remain possible. This makes traffic safer for everyone.

164362.explanation: D66 focuses on good public transport. Good public transport must be affordable and accessible, also for people on a tight budget. That should not be reserved only for those aged 67 and over.

164363.explanation: D66 advocates for more safe and continuous cycling routes. We have in mind bike streets, segregated cycle paths, and safe crossing points where these improve traffic safety and flow significantly.

164364.explanation: D66 wants Het Klooster to remain easily accessible for employees and businesses. Improvements should mainly come through shared mobility and smart public transport, not more car traffic. D66 also wants safe and good cycling routes to Het Klooster.

164367.explanation: D66 wants a healthy living environment. We focus on prevention and a healthy lifestyle. According to D66, advertisements for unhealthy food do not belong in places frequented by many young people, such as schools, public transport stops, swimming pools, and sports halls.

164303.explanation: Not automatically. The plan must be tested against fixed criteria that apply to all such plans. The Geins Geluk subsidy could be used for this.

164304.explanation: We are not an expensive, but also a thrifty municipality. We find it important that local taxes are not raised in order to maintain residents' purchasing power. Efficiency measures can always be considered first. At the same time, core municipal tasks and services must be able to be carried out. Therefore, we prefer sharp and realistic budgeting over many expensive ambitions that are unaffordable or cannot be implemented.

164305.explanation: Cooperation can be more efficient and cheaper. For example, we already cooperate in the areas of waste, work & income, and healthcare.

164306.explanation: The municipality does a lot in the field of participation. A citizens’ assembly can be a useful 'tool' to gather input from society. But it costs a lot of time and money, so it shouldn’t be used too often. It also creates expectations that cannot always be fulfilled.

164307.explanation: Much is already handled digitally. There is already evening opening, and we do not receive complaints that this is insufficient.

164309.explanation: There are also dog walking areas. Dogs cost the municipality money.

164310.explanation: The economy in Nieuwegein is doing well, and with the business park Het Klooster, we have already attracted many companies to Nieuwegein. Nieuwegein is attractive due to its central location in the country and good accessibility. We prefer to spend money on other matters such as poverty reduction or cleaner neighborhoods.

164311.explanation: In recent years, a lot has been invested in business parks in Nieuwegein. Additionally, many offices have been converted into homes. We must use the space we have carefully. The CDA prefers to focus on more housing rather than expanding business parks.

164312.explanation: Collective energy generation can simply be a private initiative. We support residents who want to generate sustainable energy. This can also be facilitated by making regulations and procedures simpler.

164314.explanation: This has already been tried in the past, but there is no operator willing to establish themselves.

164316.explanation: Worldhave owns City Plaza and is a commercial company. They charge high rents. From the municipality, we want them to lower rents so that local entrepreneurs can once again get the chance to establish themselves in City Plaza, which is now almost impossible. So, it is up to Worldhave to lower the rents.

164317.explanation: An amendment was submitted on this and it was almost unanimously adopted by the council. Every tree contributes to climate adaptation, biodiversity, and countering heat stress. Besides the aesthetic reasons for trees.

164318.explanation: It is bad for the environment, but it cannot be enforced if we ban it.

164319.explanation: Only then do we ensure that we meet the climate target and it forces companies to invest in sustainability themselves.

164320.explanation: Collecting bulky waste was already free in the past. It can actually help prevent illegal dumping and keep Nieuwegein cleaner.

164321.explanation: Many residents use weekends to clear out their belongings. Saturdays are therefore often very busy.

164322.explanation: Park Oudegein is a park. Events should not be organized there. There are sufficient other locations where this can and already does happen. Damaging greenery and biodiversity is unwise given the current climate issues.

164323.explanation: This is not a core task of the municipality. Creating gardens can also simply be a private initiative.

164325.explanation: A cleaner climate is better for our health. Because of greenhouse gases, the earth warms up, which among other things causes more extreme weather conditions. We want to keep our planet livable, and as a municipality, you can also contribute to that.

164327.explanation: We support humane shelter for people who flee war, violence, human rights violations, or persecution based on political beliefs, origin, religion, or orientation. These people deserve to sleep under a decent roof and not outside, in tents, or sports halls.

164328.explanation: Good integration is a foundation for success. Integration (civic integration, language acquisition, education, and guidance towards work) helps newcomers build a new home and strengthens social involvement.

164329.explanation: Besides a legal duty, we also have a social and humane obligation to accommodate people who have fled. Of course, all municipalities have this obligation to contribute. In Nieuwegein, we have been successfully sheltering asylum seekers for years.

164331.explanation: That already happens. Music is good for children's development. But there are sufficient parents who can afford it themselves.

164332.explanation: The municipality already subsidizes via De KOM. Many parents can pay for it themselves. Sport and music lessons can already be used with the city pass.

164336.explanation: We have little or no camera surveillance. Supervision at hotspots causes the hotspot to shift. Temporarily, it might help.

164337.explanation: We fight against every form of violence. We support bodycams, but not batons and pepper spray. There must remain a separation between police tasks and those of public order officials. Equipping them with batons and pepper spray gives public order officials greater powers to use force.

164341.explanation: 30% would be sufficient for us as well.

164343.explanation: Nieuwegein has been transforming offices into homes for years. Developers earn a lot of money from this transformation. It is important that issuing permits is not a bureaucratic and complicated procedure.

164344.explanation: We are against every form of drugs.

164345.explanation: There are many bad examples of subdividing homes, including by slumlords and companies that cram many migrant workers into one house. We oppose this, even though the housing shortage is severe. We prefer to focus on building new homes and promoting turnover in the housing market.

164348.explanation: Phasing out gas is a social task. We do find it important that residents have freedom of choice and that the transition is feasible and affordable. This does not mean the transition should be made solely for financial reasons.

164349.explanation: Life is becoming increasingly expensive. By raising the threshold, life becomes somewhat easier for people actually living in poverty.

164350.explanation: The CDA wants to encourage everyone to contribute to society, no matter how small. Unless physical or mental limitations prevent it. Therefore, there is always a tailored approach.

164351.explanation: Long-term unemployment is a social problem. The municipality can set a good example by subsidizing jobs. This gives people a meaningful existence and personal growth again.

164355.explanation: Businesses must be able to supply themselves. Polluting diesel vehicles are increasingly being replaced by electric cars.

164356.explanation: This is stated in our election program. This way, we remain competitive in the region.

164357.explanation: The CDA values traffic safety in neighborhoods. Reducing the speed limit to 30 km/h can help with this. On the H-structure (AC Verhoefweg, Structuurbaan, Buizerdlaan, Zuidstedeweg, etc.) speeds of 50-70 km/h are allowed.

164362.explanation: People aged 67+ are not poor. They are the richest generation. Why would you want this? Through the city pass, everyone who cannot afford it has access to free public transport. That is much fairer.

164364.explanation: Much has already been invested in infrastructure and Klooster is therefore easily accessible.

164367.explanation: Research shows that fast-food advertisements contribute to unhealthy eating habits. Overweight and obesity are major social problems. The CDA finds it important to encourage healthy choices (e.g., sports, healthy eating).

164303.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes in the strength of society. Residents know best what their neighborhood needs. By financially supporting neighborhood initiatives, we encourage people to care for each other and take responsibility together. This way we build a connected and vital city.

164305.explanation: We want important decisions about Nieuwegein to be made in our own municipal council so that residents retain influence. Cooperation with other municipalities is sometimes necessary for quality, but this must never come at the expense of human scale and democratic control.

164306.explanation: We believe in the strength of society. Residents often know best what is happening in their neighborhood. A citizen's assembly ensures we reach widely supported solutions for major challenges, such as climate or housing.

164307.explanation: The government should serve the citizen, not the other way around. In a modern society where many people work during the day, limited opening hours are an unnecessary barrier for arranging basic matters like a passport. Extended hours in the evening or weekend demonstrate service.

164309.explanation: We are frustrated by nuisance caused by dog feces, especially around playgrounds. A dog tax is not an end in itself for us but can only be considered if the money is directly used for enforcement and additional trash bins. We do not want a 'tax on companionship' but do want to see responsibility.

164310.explanation: Nieuwegein is already a top location because of its central position. Expensive promotional campaigns are an unnecessary use of taxpayers' money. The ChristenUnie prefers to invest this money in the quality of our neighborhood shopping centers and a good match between local job seekers and existing companies.

164311.explanation: Space is scarce. Instead of simply expanding, the ChristenUnie chooses to make more intensive use of existing areas. We want to keep the local economy but with attention to greenery and livability. Quality and sustainable use prevail over quantity.

164312.explanation: The ChristenUnie supports local energy initiatives where appropriate. Support is more than money: it also involves favorable loans and help with permits. By relieving residents, we make sustainability accessible for everyone. We simplify and tackle issues so the city remains vital.

164314.explanation: We prefer to invest in social meeting places that contribute to neighborhood connection rather than large-scale nightlife with risks of nuisance.

164316.explanation: City Plaza is managed by a large commercial party. The ChristenUnie wants the municipality to increase pressure on Wereldhave. We want the municipality to keep talking to arrange fair spaces for local entrepreneurs.

164317.explanation: Trees are crucial for clean air and cooling. The ChristenUnie stands for good stewardship: for every tree we cut down, we plant a new one. This way, we maintain a green city and tackle heat stress. We must prevent greenery from making way for concrete.

164318.explanation: We want residents to continue enjoying their homes, but health and the interests of neighbors with respiratory complaints weigh heavily. Therefore, we focus on awareness, the 'Stookwijzer' (burning guide), and addressing excessive nuisance instead of a total ban.

164319.explanation: Nieuwegein has little space, so we prefer to allocate it to companies that are good for the climate and neighborhood. We do not want to prohibit new companies but do want to encourage sustainable building and working.

164320.explanation: We want a clean Nieuwegein without litter and illegal dumping. Since the introduction of fees for bulky waste, more waste has ended up on the street; therefore, we want to make collection free again to keep neighborhoods tidy.

164322.explanation: Park Oudegein is a place for peace, nature, and relaxation. We want to protect this green heart and oppose large-scale events that cause nuisance, waste, and damage to nature. Small-scale neighborhood activities are allowed.

164323.explanation: We support residents who green the city. For small initiatives such as façade gardens, a small contribution should be possible. For larger plans like green roofs, we focus on favorable loans. This way, we make greening affordable and encourage personal responsibility.

164327.explanation: We are for dignified shelter and proactive integration.

164328.explanation: We are for dignified shelter and proactive integration.

164329.explanation: We stand for international solidarity and humane shelter where Nieuwegein takes its responsibility. However, for us, the emphasis is not on numbers but on guaranteeing quality shelter and proactive integration into our society.

164331.explanation: Music education enriches every child's development. However, the ChristenUnie believes municipal support should be targeted at children who otherwise cannot afford it. We want to address this via targeted arrangements such as the city pass, so talent is not lost due to a tight budget.

164332.explanation: We want every child to be able to play sports or take music lessons but choose targeted financial support for families who cannot afford this themselves. A universal allowance for all children is inefficient; we prefer to use our resources to truly remove barriers for those in poverty.

164336.explanation: We want as few cameras as possible, but sometimes they are needed to maintain order.

164337.explanation: We believe enforcement officers should be able to work safely, where a bodycam can help de-escalate situations. We are cautious about heavier means such as pepper spray or batons.

164341.explanation: In addition to social housing, we strongly focus on affordable homes for the middle segment so that starters and families can move up.

164343.explanation: Vacancy is a waste. The ChristenUnie wants to continue tackling transformation into homes. Through targeted support, we can set affordability requirements, such as 30% low-rent homes for starters. Without this guidance, developers often build only luxury apartments. This way, we keep the city accessible.

164344.explanation: We are for protecting vulnerable residents and promoting public health.

164345.explanation: We support private room rentals (such as hosts) to alleviate housing shortages and stimulate social connections. However, we reject commercial subdivision by investors because this harms livability in our neighborhoods and displaces starters on the housing market.

164348.explanation: We want the transition to gas-free to be affordable and feasible for everyone, with extra support to vulnerable households via the Insulation Brigade. We strive for cost-neutrality in housing expenses, so the bill for sustainability is not solely passed on to residents.

164349.explanation: The Christian Union also wants to support the "working poor." By raising the income threshold, we prevent families just above the social minimum from falling between the cracks. We want to tackle poverty and maintain security of existence for a larger group of residents.

164350.explanation: We believe every person has talents and want to encourage participation, but this must always be based on customization and voluntariness.

164351.explanation: Everyone has talent and deserves a place to use it. For those who cannot make the step to work on their own, subsidized jobs are a solution. The Christian Union wants to address this because work provides dignity and participation. This way, we keep a city that gives up on no one.

164355.explanation: Clean air is crucial for health (stewardship). However, an environmental zone must not lead to transport poverty for low-income residents. We want to tackle air quality but with fair transition arrangements and attention to the human scale.

164356.explanation: The Christian Union wants a vital city center. Free parking encourages local shopping but also attracts more traffic (environment). Moreover, it is very costly. We would rather spend this money on primary needs such as (preventive) care and poverty reduction among our residents.

164357.explanation: Safety comes first: in residential neighborhoods and near schools, 30 km/h is the norm. For the main thoroughfares in Nieuwegein, however, we want to keep 50 km/h to ensure smooth flow for emergency services and public transport. Tailored solutions per road are better than a general ban.

164362.explanation: We support free public transport specifically for low-income elderly to increase mobility and prevent loneliness.

164363.explanation: The Christian Union chooses the cyclist, but current practice can sometimes improve. A bicycle street is only successful if cars are truly guests. We want to address this by making the layout safer and banning through traffic. Safety takes priority over car speed.

164364.explanation: We want Het Klooster to be well and safely accessible for all employees, but we prioritize investments in fast bicycle routes and high-quality public transport.

164367.explanation: We want our children to grow up in an environment that encourages healthy living. By limiting advertising for unhealthy food in places where many young people gather, we help parents and children make healthier choices.

164304.explanation: Nieuwegein is a municipality with relatively low property tax (ozb). If we want to maintain the level of services, we will need to raise the property tax in line with inflation.

164305.explanation: Collaborations are cheaper and enable learning from each other.

164306.explanation: The opinion of residents is important.

164307.explanation: There is already an evening opening.

164309.explanation: Dog tax has already been abolished and we do not want to reintroduce it.

164311.explanation: There are sufficient industrial areas, and some are being transformed into residential areas.

164312.explanation: If residents want it, it should be possible, but a subsidy would be unfair to other residents.

164314.explanation: Young people should be able to go out in Nieuwegein.

164316.explanation: Vacancy is disastrous for a shopping center and the attractiveness of the municipality.

164317.explanation: At least one tree, additional planting is desirable, preferably in their own neighborhood!

164319.explanation: The basis for land use is the environmental plan, and this cannot be regulated within it.

164320.explanation: This prevents illegal dumping of waste.

164321.explanation: As an experiment, a trial period and evaluation to see if there is really a need.

164322.explanation: Park Oudegein must remain as it is. We support smaller events in neighborhoods; this is good for social cohesion.

164325.explanation: Nieuwegein wants to be energy-neutral by 2040.

164327.explanation: Status holders in Nieuwegein, who have spent years in asylum seeker centers with no space at all, should be given priority on the waiting list for suitable housing.

164328.explanation: You can always contact the municipality.

164329.explanation: We must fulfill our duty and quickly integrate asylum seekers.

164331.explanation: Free for residents with a city pass.

164332.explanation: Families with children up to 14 years old who have insufficient funds qualify for a city pass with substantial discounts for sports and music lessons.

164337.explanation: Bodycams are okay; batons and pepper spray belong with the police.

164341.explanation: Nieuwegein currently has a policy of 30% social rental housing and this should remain. However, we do focus on more housing with low middle rents.

164343.explanation: As far as possible, this is already being done.

164344.explanation: Under strict conditions, to reduce easy access of our youth to the drug world.

164345.explanation: Where possible, but with attention to excessive nuisance.

164349.explanation: There must be much more attention to hidden poverty.

164350.explanation: No obligation; volunteer work is a good alternative to remain involved in society.

164355.explanation: Logistics (delivery services) must not be polluting. Small businesses can receive an exemption.

164356.explanation: So that people are more willing to also stay longer in the City.

164357.explanation: We want a 30 km/h speed limit in residential neighborhoods, and a 70 km/h speed limit on through roads.

164362.explanation: Seniors already have a 65+ discount if they have a personal public transport card. Not every 67+ person has insufficient funds. For those who do have insufficient funds, the Nieuwegein city pass could be a solution.

164363.explanation: Pilot with more streets inside the neighborhoods.

164364.explanation: This site is easily accessible, located near the A27. Also ensure good public transport for people without cars, including at night.

164367.explanation: We are aiming for a healthy municipality.

164303.explanation: We, as ieders Belang, are always there to improve neighborhoods.

164304.explanation: The property tax (OZB) in Nieuwegein is on average slightly lower than the national average. We want to maintain this to keep our municipality attractive for companies wishing to establish here and for residents to continue living here.

164305.explanation: We should cooperate more to learn from each other and thus create better and more affordable plans for the future.

164314.explanation: Nieuwegein must be an attractive city for both young and old.

164316.explanation: We let the market do its job here; if properties are not rented out, prices will go down. It cannot be that municipal funds go to the owners of City Plaza.

164318.explanation: Residents of Nieuwegein should have the freedom to choose which energy source they use to heat their homes.

164319.explanation: We do not want to exclude companies in Nieuwegein just because they don’t focus on sustainability. Companies might also establish here for social reasons.

164320.explanation: In recent years, we have seen more illegal dumping of bulky waste in our neighborhoods. Cleaning this up costs more money than the free collection by appointment.

164323.explanation: We strongly support improving one's own neighborhood/district.

164331.explanation: Only if children want to participate and parents cannot afford it, for example through an N-pass.

164332.explanation: Not every family needs this; it can be arranged better through a Nieuwegein pass.

164336.explanation: We consider camera surveillance important, for example to solve crimes as quickly as possible.

164337.explanation: However, we support a test of this.

164350.explanation: We basically agree with this, but it requires tailored solutions. Not everyone on social assistance can do this.

164351.explanation: We leave this to the free market.

164355.explanation: There are not yet enough alternatives, think for example of market trucks.

164362.explanation: We advocate free public transport for residents who cannot afford it, not just because they have reached a certain age.

164367.explanation: We comply with national regulations here.

164304.explanation: The additional 10% increase collected by the current administration was completely unnecessary, as revealed during the budget discussion in November 2025.

164305.explanation: Collaboration with other municipalities should make everything cheaper and more efficient. The opposite happens. Just look at the waste everywhere. You pay more and more waste disposal fees, yet the environment is deteriorating further. Nieuwegein even suffers from a rat plague.

164307.explanation: The civil affairs department is not a supermarket where you do your daily shopping. The current opening hours meet the needs of residents very well. There is no good reason to spend extra money on evening and weekend openings.

164310.explanation: Establishment of new companies brings many benefits, such as extra employment and strengthening the local economy. We consider that important.

164311.explanation: In principle yes, because of the many advantages new companies bring. Each case should be assessed to determine which companies are allowed and which are not.

164312.explanation: By generating sustainable energy, you already save on your own energy costs. Until January 1, 2027, you can offset your generated electricity against your consumption. After that, you can receive a compensation for feeding surplus energy back. Therefore, there is no reason for financial support from the municipality.

164316.explanation: That is entirely up to the market and not the municipality. The community should not pay for the risks taken by an entrepreneur. It would be different if the vacancy was replaced by housing.

164320.explanation: We already predicted that charging extra for bulky waste collection was very unwise. The consequence would be more frequent illegal dumping. Waste collection has never been free. We already pay a high waste disposal fee. The extra charge must be reversed.

164321.explanation: Saturdays are often very busy. Opening on Sundays would meet the needs of many residents and reduce Saturday crowds.

164322.explanation: The decision-making around that plan is very questionable. The honest thing is to cancel the plan.

164325.explanation: We cannot control the climate in Nieuwegein or in the Netherlands. The climate always changes due to forces from the universe, such as the sun's power. We cannot compete with that. But we can do much more for the environment and air quality.

164327.explanation: Ensuring everyone has a roof over their head is a primary task of the government. But giving priority to status holders is discrimination. We find that unfair to housing seekers who have been waiting a long time for social housing.

164328.explanation: A lot is already being invested in the integration of newcomers. Spending even more money means raising more taxes from ordinary citizens, and life is already becoming increasingly expensive for those citizens.

164329.explanation: Nieuwegein faces many big city problems, such as drug-related crime, street insecurity, and deterioration of public spaces. The reception of asylum seekers places too much pressure on society. This also fails to send the message to The Hague to quickly limit the inflow.

164331.explanation: It is up to parents to pay for private lessons for their children. If it turns out that parents of talented children cannot afford the lessons, ways can be sought, such as special funds, to pay for them. The municipality is not primarily responsible for this.

164332.explanation: There is little point in throwing money at things children are not interested in. Children will find out at school whether they enjoy taking sports and music lessons outside school hours. It is then up to the parents to encourage that further.

164336.explanation: Cameras do not prevent crime. They can occasionally contribute to investigation, but that does not outweigh the privacy violations of ordinary people. We all have the right to go about our lives without being spied on.

164337.explanation: Community enforcement officers can make an important contribution to the safety feeling of neighborhood residents. But they should not become cheap police officers. We think it is important to clearly draw the line. In dangerous situations, the officer can quickly call the police.

164343.explanation: Converting office buildings can be well financed by wealthy market parties such as investors and developers. Given the current housing crisis, one can hardly speak of risks.

164344.explanation: It has been proven that opening a coffeeshop does not contribute to anyone’s well-being. Coffeeshops do lower the threshold for drug use, which we find undesirable.

164345.explanation: We must seize every opportunity to ease pressure on the housing market. Renting out single rooms can help, because in the short term the housing crisis cannot be solved by new construction.

164348.explanation: That seems quite logical to us. Life is already very expensive and residents should not be burdened with costs because of council plans.

164350.explanation: There is often a good reason why people end up on welfare. We do not support forcing people to do socially useful work against their will. Guiding them toward paid employment seems more sensible.

164351.explanation: People who are long-term unemployed but cannot get a job, for example due to a disability, get the chance to participate in the workforce again this way.

164356.explanation: Having a car is unfortunately becoming increasingly expensive. But we cannot make the community bear the costs a car owner must pay to own a car and use the garage.

164357.explanation: In residential neighborhoods, children, the elderly, and people with mobility issues walk and play, so caution is necessary. Setting a speed limit can help with this.

164364.explanation: Accessibility of business parks is a shared responsibility and they must work together on it. It cannot be entirely left to the municipality or the community.

164367.explanation: We support healthy eating but see no benefit in selectively banning certain advertisements. That is discrimination. Education for organizations, parents, and children will be much more effective.

164303.explanation: It is great when people take this initiative using their own resources. If municipal funds need to be used, initiatives can be brought up with the parties in the city council.

164304.explanation: The property tax infringes on property rights. This tax is immoral.

164305.explanation: We believe the municipality should always prioritize its own residents. Partnerships can pressure this by requiring concessions.

164306.explanation: It is important to get as much input from residents as possible because the municipality can then make better decisions that address the issues people face.

164307.explanation: This would especially be beneficial for working people.

164309.explanation: Taxes are rising on all fronts. We believe people should not be additionally taxed for owning a dog.

164310.explanation: That does not always benefit the residents of Nieuwegein. Let's focus first on the entrepreneurs who are already here and make it easier for them to run their businesses.

164311.explanation: It is good for the economy and employment in Nieuwegein.

164312.explanation: We believe tax money should not go toward producing a specific form of energy. People may pay for that themselves if they want to.

164314.explanation: We are not against night-time hospitality, but it should not be subsidized with tax money.

164316.explanation: That is too much interference in the market, causing the community to pay for rent in the private sector.

164317.explanation: That is too simplistic. We want to green the municipality, but it does not have to be done with quotas. That can be done more intelligently.

164318.explanation: We cannot expect people not to use alternative heating methods, especially given gas prices. Moreover, this usually does not cause nuisance.

164319.explanation: Sustainability is not well enough defined in this statement.

164320.explanation: That will help create a clean living environment and reduce litter on the streets.

164321.explanation: This will help keep the city clean. Some people have a busy workweek and it would help them a lot to be able to come on Sundays. It also makes other days less crowded at the waste center.

164322.explanation: Park Oudegein is a small natural area where residents can find peace. We do not support taking away that last piece of nature and tranquility from residents.

164323.explanation: It is great if people arrange this on their own initiative, but not with tax money.

164325.explanation: This is far too expensive, unfeasible, and Nieuwegein has no impact on global warming.

164327.explanation: We believe the municipality should not give priority to non-Dutch people for social rental homes. Many Dutch people have been waiting for years, and it is not fair to disadvantage them compared to people who have recently arrived in the Netherlands.

164328.explanation: We are not convinced that the municipality will have much positive impact on the integration of newcomers in society. Meanwhile, the money could be spent differently.

164329.explanation: That is not in the interest of the residents of Nieuwegein, among other reasons due to housing.

164331.explanation: It would be valuable for the musical and cultural development of children to be able to get music lessons, even if their parents have little money.

164332.explanation: That is a bit too simplistic. Perhaps the municipality can offer a discount for sports and music lessons to children up to 14 years old, if they want to make use of it.

164336.explanation: We want to facilitate camera surveillance only at locations where there is a lot of nuisance, for example. We always want to take the privacy of residents into account.

164337.explanation: A bodycam can help prevent disputes about what was said or happened. However, we believe there must remain a clear distinction between police officers and public enforcement officers (boa’s). Equipping boa’s with batons and pepper spray blurs that distinction too much.

164341.explanation: The government interferes too much with the housing market. It is important that people also have ownership, because generally they take better care of their homes.

164343.explanation: We want to facilitate housing as long as no market distortion occurs and the housing benefits the residents of Nieuwegein.

164344.explanation: We believe there are enough coffeeshops near Nieuwegein and although we are not anti-coffeeshop, we think it is not in the municipality’s interest to have one in Nieuwegein.

164345.explanation: We want to allow landlords to rent out individual rooms. In general, we think it is better for the government to interfere as little as possible in the housing market. Allowing this also creates more housing availability in Nieuwegein for people who want to rent a room.

164348.explanation: We want to keep the option available for people to use natural gas. This is also safer because we do not know what the future holds.

164350.explanation: It is fair to give something back to society in exchange for social assistance benefits that society provides.

164351.explanation: We do not believe in creating jobs just for the sake of creating jobs. It is unwise to apply a quota for this.

164355.explanation: The accessibility of Nieuwegein must not be compromised because of introducing an environmental zone. Moreover, it does not yield any benefits.

164356.explanation: It must remain attractive for people who only stay briefly near City Plaza so that they can easily get there. A short visit has little impact on the number of parking spaces available.

164357.explanation: That is too simplistic. There are places within the built-up area where the speed can safely be more than 30 km/h without compromising safety.

164363.explanation: We want to maintain good accessibility for everyone.

164364.explanation: In general, we are pro-car drivers and want to facilitate people who have to go somewhere by car as much as possible. If accessibility to that area poses a problem, we are happy to think along about solutions to improve accessibility.

164367.explanation: Everyone is responsible for their own body and mind. We do not believe the municipality should interfere with people's personal health.

statements.title: The first hour of parking in the City Plaza parking garage should be free.
statements.moreInfo: Parking in the City Plaza parking garage now costs €3.30 per hour.

statements.title: The municipality should invest in a promotional campaign for Nieuwegein as an attractive location for businesses.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in een

titleParts.glossary: A series of advertisements on, for example, billboards, television, and social media.

titleParts.text: voor Nieuwegein als aantrekkelijke vestigingsplaats voor bedrijven.

statements.title: There should be less camera surveillance in Nieuwegein.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.moreInfo: Camera surveillance refers to the use of cameras in public places, such as streets and squares. Footage of criminal offenses is shared with the police.

statements.title: The municipality should handle more itself and participate less in partnerships with other municipalities.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality has many tasks. Most Dutch municipalities work together to carry out those tasks. The aldermen and civil servants then consult with each other to arrange the cooperation. A decision by such a cooperative body then applies to all municipalities participating in it.

statements.title: By 2040 Nieuwegein should emit no greenhouse gases so the municipality no longer contributes to climate warming.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The civil affairs counter at Nieuwegein City Hall should be open more often in the evenings and at weekends.
statements.moreInfo: The Civil Affairs counter is open every day between 8:30 and 17:00. On Wednesdays the counter is open until 20:00. The counter is closed on weekends.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.moreInfo: Homeowners can install solar panels on their roof if the roof is suitable, or apply for a permit for a wind turbine in the garden if it is large enough. If that is not possible, people can buy or rent land together with others for one or more wind turbines or solar panels so they can still invest in renewable energy. Renewable energy is generated from sources that never run out, such as sunlight and wind.

statements.title: The municipality should financially contribute to lower rents at City Plaza to prevent vacancies.
The plan for the event site in Park Oudegein should go ahead.
statements.moreInfo: The municipal council of Nieuwegein decided in April 2024 that there will be an event field on the large meadow in Park Oudegein. The plans are not yet final; there are still studies and public consultation moments planned before construction begins.

statements.title: Nieuwegein should accommodate more asylum seekers than legally required.

titleParts.glossary: People who have applied for asylum and are still waiting for a decision.

statements.title: The municipality should disconnect homes from natural gas only if residents gain a financial benefit.
Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The BOAs who enforce safety in Nieuwegein should be given a baton, pepper spray and a bodycam.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Nieuwegein controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok, pepperspray en een bodycam krijgen.

statements.moreInfo: The municipality is responsible for enforcement of minor offenses, for example parking without paying, cycling on the pavement or littering in nature. People who do this receive a fine. The municipality's enforcement officers are boa's (special investigation officers). Police officers are general investigation officers and have different tasks and powers. There are also boa's who do not work for the municipality, for example on trains.

statements.title: The municipality should give every child up to 14 years old in Nieuwegein one hundred euros per year for sports and music lessons.
The municipality should spend more money on integrating newcomers into society.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld uitgeven aan

titleParts.glossary: Newcomers learn the language and customs of the Netherlands.
People who are new to the Netherlands.

statements.title: Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.
The municipality should make the Het Klooster business park more accessible by car.
There should be at least twenty subsidized jobs for long-term unemployed people in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Government-funded jobs for people who have little chance of getting a regular job.

titleParts.text: voor langdurig werklozen komen in onze gemeente.

statements.moreInfo: People who have no work and no other benefits can apply to the municipality for a social assistance benefit. There are conditions and rules for this; for example, you must apply for jobs regularly. Nieuwegein has already run a small-scale trial with a basic income. People who qualified for social assistance received the benefit without having to follow rules in return. They therefore did not have to apply for jobs.

statements.title: The recycling center (the municipal depot) should be open on Sundays.
statements.moreInfo: The recycling station is open on weekdays from 9:00 to 16:00 (on Mondays from 10:00). On Saturdays it is open between 9:00 and 16:30 (May–July until 17:30).

statements.title: The municipality should ban fast-food advertising in bus shelters, swimming pools and sports halls.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

titleParts.text: krijgen, moeten in ruil daarvoor maatschappelijk zinvol werk doen.

statements.moreInfo: People who have no work and no other benefit can apply to the municipality for social assistance. In some municipalities they are required to perform a counter-performance. They then do socially useful work for which they are not paid.

statements.title: The speed limit within built-up areas must be set to 30 km/h everywhere.

titleParts.glossary: An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: Business parks in Nieuwegein should be allowed to expand.
The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

shootoutStatements.title: Nieuwegein should introduce a low-emission zone to keep polluting vehicles out of the center.
shootoutStatements.moreInfo: Sommige steden hebben een milieuzone, meestal in de binnenstad.  Voertuigen die veel schadelijke stoffen uitstoten mogen de zone dan niet meer inrijden. Vaak gaat het om vrachtwagens, busjes en auto’s die op diesel rijden. Sommige gemeenten maken hun binnenstad autoluw. Er mogen dan nog minder voertuigen rondrijden. In een autovrij gebied mogen auto’s, busjes en vrachtwagens helemaal niet komen.

shootoutStatements.title: The municipality should convert more streets into bicycle streets, such as Herenstraat and Vreeswijksestraatweg.
Residents who have a plan to improve their neighborhood should receive funding from the municipality for that.
In Nieuwegein the use of fireplaces and wood stoves should remain allowed.
Only companies that operate sustainably should be allowed to establish themselves in our municipality from now on.
The municipality should more often establish a citizens' assembly that advises on important issues.
The municipality must introduce a dog tax.
Residents who plan to create neighbourhood, façade or rooftop gardens should receive funding from the municipality.
People aged 67 and over should be able to travel free on public transport in Nieuwegein and the surrounding area.
For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
The municipality should work to bring nightlife venues to Nieuwegein, such as a nightclub.
At least 40% of the newly built homes in Nieuwegein should be social rental housing. In the Netherlands, 28% of homes are currently social rental housing.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
shootoutStatements.moreInfo: In Nieuwegein krijgen inwoners alleen financiële hulp als zij minder verdienen dan 120% van de bijstandsnorm: het bedrag dat de overheid minimaal nodig vindt om van te kunnen leven. Voor alleenstaande volwassenen is dit bedrag in 2026 ongeveer € 1.400 en voor samenwonenden ongeveer € 2.000 per maand. Wil de gemeente meer mensen helpen, dan kan zij de inkomensgrens verhogen (bijvoorbeeld naar 130% van de bijstandsnorm).
